requirement_summary: Every morning, operations staff manually review unshipped Amazon.de FBM orders, retrieve order items, separate orders by SKU and quantity patterns, prepare picking and packing documents, and generate shipping labels. This manual process is time-consuming, creates duplicate-work risk, and leads to fulfillment mistakes when orders contain mixed SKUs, repeated products, or require special review. There is no structured workflow or tooling to automate the preparation phase of FBM order fulfillment.
architecture_summary: Implement the first release as a browser-only single-page application that loads embedded mock Amazon.de FBM order data at startup, derives all grouping and review indicators client-side, persists reviewed or processed state in browser storage with in-memory fallback, and presents a responsive dashboard optimized for quick operational scanning. The architecture should separate domain logic from presentation so the current mock-data implementation can later be swapped with real service adapters without redesigning the UI. No server-side components, live APIs, authentication flows, or Azure-hosted runtime dependencies are required for the delivered slice; Azure guidance is provided only for optional static hosting and future extensibility after human review.

architecture_decisions.decision: Use a client-only SPA architecture with embedded mock data and no backend for the initial release.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Satisfies constraints for browser-only, mock-only, zero external runtime dependencies.

architecture_decisions.decision: Isolate grouping, review-flagging, summary, and state-transition rules into pure domain modules independent of UI components.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Improves testability and enables future replacement of mock provider with real API adapter.

architecture_decisions.decision: Persist reviewed/processed state using browser local storage with fallback to in-memory session state.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Respects no-backend constraint while providing persistence across refreshes.

architecture_decisions.decision: Model dashboard group membership as a single deterministic primary bucket per order, with independent review flags and processed status overlays.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Ensures each order appears in exactly one category for clarity while surfacing separate concerns.

component_model.name: app_shell
component_model.responsibility: Bootstrap, top-level layout, orchestration of data loading and panels.

component_model.name: mock_order_data_provider
component_model.responsibility: Supply embedded mock FBM orders with schema validation.

component_model.name: order_classification_engine
component_model.responsibility: Compute grouping bucket, review flags, repeated-SKU detection, summaries.

component_model.name: order_state_store
component_model.responsibility: Manage reviewed/processed state, filters, sorting, reset.

component_model.name: dashboard_view
component_model.responsibility: Render grouped sections, summary cards, review indicators, status badges.

component_model.name: order_details_panel
component_model.responsibility: Expandable item-level details (SKU, name, qty, price).

repo_structure_guidance.purpose: Pure order domain logic (grouping, review, summaries)

implementation_slices.title: slice_1_bootstrap_mock_data_and_domain_types
implementation_slices.description: SPA scaffold, typed models, embedded mock data, provider abstraction.

implementation_slices.title: slice_2_grouping_and_manual_review_engine
implementation_slices.description: Deterministic grouping rules, repeated-SKU detection, review flags, summary calculations as pure functions.

implementation_slices.title: slice_3_dashboard_and_order_details_ui
implementation_slices.description: Grouped dashboard, summary cards, status badges, expandable order details, responsive layout.

implementation_slices.title: slice_4_review_processed_state_and_duplicate_prevention
implementation_slices.description: Local state management, persistence, duplicate-prevention, visible state changes, reset.

implementation_slices.title: slice_5_activity_log_filters_and_sorting
implementation_slices.description: Activity log, filtering, sorting controls on derived views.

risks: Ambiguity in high-quantity threshold for review flags
Grouping precedence for repeated-SKU orders not yet explicitly defined
Local storage availability varies across environments
Activity log may be mistaken for permanent audit trail
Responsive dashboard may be visually dense on tablets
assumptions: First slice is a human-reviewed demo app, not production
Default high-quantity threshold is a configurable constant
Each mock order has a unique order_id
Future real API integration will reuse same domain contracts
open_questions: Exact quantity threshold for high-quantity review flag?
Should reviewed and processed be separate visible states?
Should grouping events be logged once at startup or recalculated on state changes?
For repeated-SKU single-SKU orders: primary group = mixed or single-SKU bucket with review badge?
Is persistence expected across browser restarts or just page refresh?

handoff.implementation_brief_markdown:
# FBM Order Grouping & Review Dashboard (Mock Data)

Browser-only SPA. Embedded mock data. No backend. Groups by SKU/qty, flags review cases, prevents duplicates. 5 slices.
handoff.notes_for_copilot: Typed SPA with pure domain modules. Mock data behind interface. Accessible expandable rows. No backend/auth/live integrations.
handoff.notes_for_cicd_agent: Static front-end build + test pipeline. Add mock-only endpoint guard.
handoff.notes_for_iac_agent: No Azure required for first slice. If hosting later: Azure Static Web Apps or Storage static website.
handoff.notes_for_test_agent: Prioritize grouping, review rules, summaries, persistence fallback, reset, accessibility. Add network-call guard test.
handoff.notes_for_reviewer: Verify browser-only + mock-only constraints. Clean domain/presentation separation. Edge cases for repeated-SKU. No production readiness claims.
